refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying 'communication quality' without prescribing how to achieve it, and it is atomic as it addresses one clear functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design redundant communication paths to provide failover and reduce effective packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control to dynamically optimize teleoperation data flow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network condition monitoring accuracy
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting transmission parameters, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. It avoids coupling with specific hardware or technologies and supports independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable network condition metrics to inform adaptation
Risk that adaptation may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness
Assumes that the agreed network conditions are stable enough for adaptation to be effective
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating monitoring and adaptation functions into distinct DPs to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management components responsible for handling communication protocols, monitoring network conditions, and adjusting transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends monitored network performance data and status updates to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with the physical and logical network layers to send and receive data packets, enabling adaptive communication adjustments.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the adaptive communication protocol logically belongs to the network gateway solution; minimal diffusion as most related functions are within this module.
Contains some communication hardware but lacks protocol adaptation logic, leading to significant diffusion outside the module.
Focuses on remote control tasks rather than communication protocol management, causing poor containment and high diffusion.
Primarily concerned with safety alerts and monitoring, not communication protocol adaptation, resulting in very weak containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it centralizes communication management functions, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion of the DP.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
If network conditions change rapidly, the module must handle timely updates without causing latency issues elsewhere.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the teleoperation communication quality requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may introduce coupling risks.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring packet loss remains ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the DP within a single module boundary responsible for communication management, providing clear allocation of the FR to the IM.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to manage dependencies and minimize coupling.
Include latency and real-time update handling requirements in the module design to address potential performance risks.
Document monitoring and reporting mechanisms via the Telemetry interface to support verification of packet loss criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear verification feasibility, ownership clarity, and acceptable diffusion risks. The DP directly addresses the FR, and the IM module encapsulates the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion. Although some coupling risks exist, they are manageable with recommended interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms. Given the high safety criticality, the current level is sufficiently detailed and verified to proceed without further decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface contracts with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, include latency and real-time update handling requirements, and document monitoring and reporting mechanisms to manage dependencies and support verification.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can implement the adaptive communication protocol effectively.
Telemetry interface can reliably monitor packet loss metrics under operational conditions.
open_risks: Potential coupling risks due to dependencies on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Performance risks related to latency and real-time updates.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms.
